title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Life Insurance for transgender: All you need to know
A life insurance policy is underwritten (risk assessment) based on various factors like -
Age
Medical history
Family history
Occupation
Gender, etc.
Gender plays a major role in underwriting since women have been found to outlive men1. Data shows that while women have a life expectancy of 79 years, men’s expectancy is 72 years1. As such, many insurance companies have differential premiums for males and females as the latter usually enjoy lower premiums than their male counterparts2.
But what about a life insurance plan for transgender? How is the risk assessed and premium determined in such cases? Let’s understand.
Life insurance for transgender - Is it possible?
Many people wonder whether life insurance is available for transgenders. The answer is ‘Yes’ it is. Nowadays, many life insurance companies have started offering their plans to transgenders3. In fact, a leading life insurance provider also offers the third option in the gender section to allow transgenders to pick the right gender when applying for a life insurance policy.
Life insurance underwriting rules for transgender applicants
When it comes to underwriting, different insurance companies have different approaches. However, underwriting the policy for transgender applicants has its fair share of challenges. These challenges are as follows3 -
Limited data
Firstly, there’s not enough data to determine the mortality risk of transgenders. While women are found to outlive men, and the mortality rates of males and females are available, the same cannot be said for transgenders. That is why underwriters might find it hard to assign a mortality premium to a transgender applicant. This might also drive up the premium because of ambiguity in data.
Possible lack of income
Though there is rising awareness about recognizing transgenders and treating them as equals, the progress rate is usually low. As such, transgenders might not find work or have limited income. As they lack income or a stable source of earning, financial underwriting becomes challenging.
Higher mortality risk
Transgenders might also have a higher mortality risk due to depression or psychological issues. There might be a risk of suicide in the community. Moreover, those that undergo gender change surgeries might develop health conditions that affect their mortality. That is why medical underwriting proves challenging for the transgender community.
Coverage options for transgenders
If an insurance company allows coverage for transgenders, usually, all types of plans are open for such individuals. Transgenders can opt for term insurance, savings-oriented plans, retirement plans, etc. However, depending on underwriting, the insurance company determines which policies it will issue to transgenders. Moreover, transgenders might also have to undergo a pre-entrance health check-up when applying for the policy.
So, transgenders seeking coverage will have to explore the coverage options available for them depending on their eligibility parameters.
Conclusion
Transgenders, as a community, have insurance needs as well. That is why many life insurance companies have started offering their products to the community. However, when buying a suitable life insurance plan, transgenders should know the terms and conditions involved. This would help them understand the coverage and the premium charged, and they will be able to enjoy the financial security that life insurance plans offer.
